name: Malabrigo
description: Family-owned indie dyer specialising in hand-dyed merino. Every skein is unique, kettle-dyed in small batches by artisans in Montevideo. Known for their Rios and Worsted lines.

name: Drops Design
description: One of Europe's largest yarn brands, offering an enormous range of affordable yarns and free patterns. Committed to responsible sourcing with GOTS and Standard 100 certifications across their range.

name: Lana Grossa
description: Premium German yarn brand with roots in Italian wool traditions. Lana Grossa specialises in fashion-forward colourways and high-end fibre blends, with a growing sustainable line called Cool Wool.

name: West Yorkshire Spinners
description: Bradford-based mill producing British wool yarns in a mill that has operated for over a century. Their Signature 4ply is beloved by sock knitters worldwide, and their fleece is 100% British wool.

name: Cascade Yarns
description: Seattle-based brand sourcing Peruvian highland wool. Their 220 worsted weight is a staple in the knitting community for its durability and wide colour range. Fair trade partnerships with Andean cooperatives.

name: Isager
description: Danish minimalist yarn brand founded by Marianne Isager. Their yarns are designed to work together in layered weights and are spun from fine Merino and linen. Beloved by Scandinavian-style knitters.

name: Rowan
description: Iconic British yarn brand based in Yorkshire, long associated with luxury knitting. Rowan publishes beautiful pattern magazines and sources wool from British and New Zealand farms. Their Felted Tweed is a community favourite.

name: Sandnes Garn
description: Norwegian yarn brand founded in 1888, one of the oldest in Scandinavia. Their Tynn Merinoull and Sunday yarns are staples for colourwork lovers. They run a transparency programme documenting their supply chain.

name: Wollmeise
description: Cult Bavarian indie dyer operating out of Munich. Claudia Wollmeise hand-dyes small batches in a huge range of complex, saturated colourways. Famous for selling out within minutes of each release.

name: Kremke Soul Wool
description: Berlin-based yarn label focused on sustainable, responsibly sourced fibres. Their ECO Cashmere and Stellaris lines are GOTS certified and spun in Europe. Packaging is 100% plastic-free.

name: Filcolana
description: Danish brand producing fine Nordic yarns, particularly known for their Peruvian Highland Wool and lace-weight Tilia. Popular with minimalist and Fair Isle knitters. Strong focus on transparency and quality.

name: BC Garn
description: Small Danish yarn company with a strong organic and ecological ethos. All yarns are GOTS certified and made from natural fibres. Their Hamelton Tweed and Soft Silk lines are particularly well regarded.

name: Noro
description: Japanese yarn brand founded by Eisaku Noro, famous for self-striping, space-dyed yarns using natural fibres like silk, wool, and mohair. The long colour gradients are entirely natural — no overdyeing.

name: Lang Yarns
description: Swiss yarn manufacturer with over 100 years of history in the textile industry. Known for precision, consistency, and a wide technical range spanning everything from fine lace to chunky. Their Merino 120 is a wardrobe staple.

name: Hedgehog Fibres
description: Cork-based indie dyer run by Bettina Welker. Hedgehog Fibres produces small-batch, hand-dyed yarns in luminous, jewel-toned colourways on bases of merino, BFL, and silk blends. A community darling and frequent Ravelry bestseller.
